verses.latin_vulgate: Consolamini, consolamini, popule meus,  dicit Deus vester.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַחֲמ֥וּ נַחֲמ֖וּ עַמִּ֑י יֹאמַ֖ר אֱלֹהֵיכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Be comforted, be comforted, my people, saith your God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Lohduttakaat, lohduttakaat minun kansaani, sanoo teidän Jumalanne.
verses.king_james: Comfort ye, comfort ye my people, saith your God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Loquimini ad cor Jerusalem,  et advocate eam,  quoniam completa est malitia ejus,  dimissa est iniquitas illius :  suscepit de manu Domini duplicia  pro omnibus peccatis suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דַּבְּר֞וּ עַל־לֵ֤ב יְרוּשָׁלַ֙͏ִם֙ וְקִרְא֣וּ אֵלֶ֔יהָ כִּ֤י מָֽלְאָה֙ צְבָאָ֔הּ כִּ֥י נִרְצָ֖ה עֲוֺנָ֑הּ כִּ֤י לָֽקְחָה֙ מִיַּ֣ד יְהֹוָ֔ה כִּפְלַ֖יִם בְּכׇל־חַטֹּאתֶֽיהָ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Speak ye to the heart of Jerusalem, and call to her: for her evil is come to an end, her iniquity is forgiven: she hath received of the hand of the Lord double for all her sins.
verses.finnish_biblia: Puhukaat suloisesti Jerusalemin kanssa, ja saarnatkaat hänelle, että hänen sotimisensa on täytetty; sillä hänen rikoksensa on annettu anteeksi, että hän on saanut kaksinkertaisesti Herran kädestä, kaikkein synteinsä tähden.
verses.king_james: Speak ye comfortably to Jerusalem, and cry unto her, that her warfare is accomplished, that her iniquity is pardoned: for she hath received of the Lord’s hand double for all her sins.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vox clamantis in deserto :  Parate viam Domini,  rectas facite in solitudine semitas Dei nostri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֣וֹל קוֹרֵ֔א בַּמִּדְבָּ֕ר פַּנּ֖וּ דֶּ֣רֶךְ יְהֹוָ֑ה יַשְּׁרוּ֙ בָּעֲרָבָ֔ה מְסִלָּ֖ה לֵאלֹהֵֽינוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The voice of one crying in the desert: Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make straight in the wilderness the paths of our God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Huutavaisen ääni on korvessa: valmistakaat Herran tietä, tehkäät tasaiset polut erämaassa meidän Jumalallemme.
verses.king_james: The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis vallis exaltabitur,  et omnis mons et collis humiliabitur,  et erunt prava in directa,  et aspera in vias planas :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־גֶּיא֙ יִנָּשֵׂ֔א וְכׇל־הַ֥ר וְגִבְעָ֖ה יִשְׁפָּ֑לוּ וְהָיָ֤ה הֶֽעָקֹב֙ לְמִישׁ֔וֹר וְהָרְכָסִ֖ים לְבִקְעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low, and the crooked shall become straight, and the rough ways plain.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki laaksot pitää korotettaman, ja kaikki vuoret ja kukkulat pitää alennettaman, ja mitä on tasoittamatta, pitää tasattaman ja koliat silitettämän.
verses.king_james: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low: and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough places plain:

verses.latin_vulgate: et revelabitur gloria Domini,  et videbit omnis caro pariter  quod os Domini locutum est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִגְלָ֖ה כְּב֣וֹד יְהֹוָ֑ה וְרָא֤וּ כׇל־בָּשָׂר֙ יַחְדָּ֔ו כִּ֛י פִּ֥י יְהֹוָ֖ה דִּבֵּֽר׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh together shall see, that the mouth of the Lord hath spoken.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Herran kunnia ilmoitetaan: ja kaikki liha on ynnä näkevä Herran suun puhuvan.
verses.king_james: And the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see it together: for the mouth of the Lord hath spoken it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vox dicentis : Clama.  Et dixi : Quid clamabo ?  Omnis caro fœnum,  et omnis gloria ejus quasi flos agri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֚וֹל אֹמֵ֣ר קְרָ֔א וְאָמַ֖ר מָ֣ה אֶקְרָ֑א כׇּל־הַבָּשָׂ֣ר חָצִ֔יר וְכׇל־חַסְדּ֖וֹ כְּצִ֥יץ הַשָּׂדֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The voice of one, saying: Cry. And I said: What shall I cry? All flesh is grass, and all the glory thereof as the flower of the field.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ääni sanoo: saarnaa. Ja hän sanoi: Mitä minun pitää saarnaaman? Kaikki liha on ruoho, ja kaikki hänen hyvyytensä niinkuin kedon kukkanen.
verses.king_james: The voice said, Cry. And he said, What shall I cry? All flesh is grass, and all the goodliness thereof is as the flower of the field:

verses.latin_vulgate: Exsiccatum est fœnum, et cecidit flos,  quia spiritus Domini sufflavit in eo.  Vere fœnum est populus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָבֵ֤שׁ חָצִיר֙ נָ֣בֵֽל־צִ֔יץ כִּ֛י ר֥וּחַ יְהֹוָ֖ה נָ֣שְׁבָה בּ֑וֹ אָכֵ֥ן חָצִ֖יר הָעָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: The grass is withered, and the flower is fallen, because the spirit of the Lord hath blown upon it. Indeed the people is grass:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ruoho kuivuu pois ja kukkanen lakastuu, sillä Herra puhalsi siihen. Kansa tosin on ruoho.
verses.king_james: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: because the spirit of the Lord bloweth upon it: surely the people is grass.

verses.latin_vulgate: exsiccatum est fœnum, et cecidit flos ;  verbum autem Domini nostri manet in æternum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָבֵ֥שׁ חָצִ֖יר נָ֣בֵֽל צִ֑יץ וּדְבַר־אֱלֹהֵ֖ינוּ יָק֥וּם לְעוֹלָֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: The grass is withered, and the flower is fallen: but the word of our Lord endureth for ever.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ruoho kuivettuu ja kukkanen lakastuu; mutta meidän Jumalamme sana pysyy ijankaikkisesti.
verses.king_james: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Super montem excelsum ascende,  tu qui evangelizas Sion ;  exalta in fortitudine vocem tuam,  qui evangelizas Jerusalem :  exalta, noli timere.  Dic civitatibus Juda :  Ecce Deus vester :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֣ל הַר־גָּבֹ֤הַּ עֲלִי־לָךְ֙ מְבַשֶּׂ֣רֶת צִיּ֔וֹן הָרִ֤ימִי בַכֹּ֙חַ֙ קוֹלֵ֔ךְ מְבַשֶּׂ֖רֶת יְרוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם הָרִ֙ימִי֙ אַל־תִּירָ֔אִי אִמְרִי֙ לְעָרֵ֣י יְהוּדָ֔ה הִנֵּ֖ה אֱלֹהֵיכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Get thee up upon a high mountain, thou that bringest good tidings to Sion: lift up thy voice with strength, thou that bringest good tidings to Jerusalem: lift it up, fear not. Say to the cities of Juda: Behold your God:
verses.finnish_biblia: Zion, sinä suloinen saarnaaja, astu korkialle vuorelle; Jerusalem, sinä suloinen saarnaaja, korota äänes väkevästi: korota ja älä pelkää; sano Juudan kaupungeille: katso, teidän Jumalanne.
verses.king_james: O Zion, that bringest good tidings, get thee up into the high mountain; O Jerusalem, that bringest good tidings, lift up thy voice with strength; lift it up, be not afraid; say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God!

verses.latin_vulgate: ecce Dominus Deus in fortitudine veniet,  et brachium ejus dominabitur :  ecce merces ejus cum eo,  et opus illius coram illo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֨ה אֲדֹנָ֤י יֱהֹוִה֙ בְּחָזָ֣ק יָב֔וֹא וּזְרֹע֖וֹ מֹ֣שְׁלָה ל֑וֹ הִנֵּ֤ה שְׂכָרוֹ֙ אִתּ֔וֹ וּפְעֻלָּת֖וֹ לְפָנָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold the Lord God shall come with strength, and his arm shall rule: Behold his reward is with him and his work is before him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä katso, Herra, Herra tulee väkevyydessä, ja hänen käsivartensa on hallitseva: katso, hänen palkkansa on hänen myötänsä, ja hänen tekonsa on hänen edessänsä,
verses.king_james: Behold, the Lord God will come with strong hand, and his arm shall rule for him: behold, his reward is with him, and his work before him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut pastor gregem suum pascet,  in brachio suo congregabit agnos,  et in sinu suo levabit ;  fœtas ipse portabit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּרֹעֶה֙ עֶדְר֣וֹ יִרְעֶ֔ה בִּזְרֹעוֹ֙ יְקַבֵּ֣ץ טְלָאִ֔ים וּבְחֵיק֖וֹ יִשָּׂ֑א עָל֖וֹת יְנַהֵֽל׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: He shall feed his flock like a shepherd: he shall gather together the lambs with his arm, and shall take them up in his bosom, and he himself shall carry them that are with young.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin paimen on hän kaitseva laumaansa, kokoo karitsat syliinsä ja kantaa helmassansa: tiineet lampaat hän johdattaa.
verses.king_james: He shall feed his flock like a shepherd: he shall gather the lambs with his arm, and carry them in his bosom, and shall gently lead those that are with young.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis mensus est pugillo aquas,  et cælos palmo ponderavit ?  quis appendit tribus digitis molem terræ,  et libravit in pondere montes,  et colles in statera ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִי־מָדַ֨ד בְּשׇׁעֳל֜וֹ מַ֗יִם וְשָׁמַ֙יִם֙ בַּזֶּ֣רֶת תִּכֵּ֔ן וְכָ֥ל בַּשָּׁלִ֖שׁ עֲפַ֣ר הָאָ֑רֶץ וְשָׁקַ֤ל בַּפֶּ֙לֶס֙ הָרִ֔ים וּגְבָע֖וֹת בְּמֹאזְנָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and weighed the heavens with his palm? who hath poised with three fingers the bulk of the earth, and weighed the mountains in scales, and the hills in a balance?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka on vedet pivollansa mitannut, ja käsittänyt vaaksallansa taivaat? ja maan tomun sulkenut kolmannekseen? ja vuoret puntarilla punninnut, ja kukkulat vaalla?
verses.king_james: Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and meted out heaven with the span, and comprehended the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales, and the hills in a balance?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis adjuvit spiritum Domini ?  aut quis consiliarius ejus fuit, et ostendit illi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־תִכֵּ֥ן אֶת־ר֖וּחַ יְהֹוָ֑ה וְאִ֖ישׁ עֲצָת֥וֹ יוֹדִיעֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who hath forwarded the spirit of the Lord? or who hath been his counsellor, and hath taught him?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka on Herran henkeä opettanut? eli kuka on hänen neuvonantajansa ollut?
verses.king_james: Who hath directed the Spirit of the Lord, or being his counsellor hath taught him?

verses.latin_vulgate: cum quo iniit consilium, et instruxit eum,  et docuit eum semitam justitiæ,  et erudivit eum scientiam,  et viam prudentiæ ostendit illi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶת־מִ֤י נוֹעָץ֙ וַיְבִינֵ֔הוּ וַֽיְלַמְּדֵ֖הוּ בְּאֹ֣רַח מִשְׁפָּ֑ט וַיְלַמְּדֵ֣הוּ דַ֔עַת וְדֶ֥רֶךְ תְּבוּנ֖וֹת יוֹדִיעֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: With whom hath he consulted, and who hath instructed him, and taught him the path of justice, and taught him knowledge, and shewed him the way of understanding?
verses.finnish_biblia: Keneltä hän neuvoa kysyy, joka hänelle ymmärrystä antais, ja opettais hänelle oikeuden tien, ja antais hänelle tiedon, ja opettais hänelle ymmärryksen tien?
verses.king_james: With whom took he counsel, and who instructed him, and taught him in the path of judgment, and taught him knowledge, and shewed to him the way of understanding?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce gentes quasi stilla situlæ,  et quasi momentum stateræ reputatæ sunt ;  ecce insulæ quasi pulvis exiguus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤ן גּוֹיִם֙ כְּמַ֣ר מִדְּלִ֔י וּכְשַׁ֥חַק מֹאזְנַ֖יִם נֶחְשָׁ֑בוּ הֵ֥ן אִיִּ֖ים כַּדַּ֥ק יִטּֽוֹל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold the Gentiles are as a drop of a bucket, and are counted as the smallest grain of a balance: behold the islands are as a little dust.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, pakanat ovat niinkuin pisara, joka jää ämpäriin, ja niinkuin rahtu, joka jää vaakaan; katso, luodot hän kuljettaa niinkuin piskuisen tomun.
verses.king_james: Behold, the nations are as a drop of a bucket, and are counted as the small dust of the balance: behold, he taketh up the isles as a very little thing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Libanus non sufficiet ad succendendum,  et animalia ejus non sufficient ad holocaustum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּלְבָנ֕וֹן אֵ֥ין דֵּ֖י בָּעֵ֑ר וְחַ֨יָּת֔וֹ אֵ֥ין דֵּ֖י עוֹלָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Libanus shall not be enough to burn, nor the beasts thereof sufficient for a burnt offering.
verses.finnish_biblia: Libanon olis sangen vähä tuleksi; ja hänen eläimensä ylen harvat polttouhriksi.
verses.king_james: And Lebanon is not sufficient to burn, nor the beasts thereof sufficient for a burnt offering.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes gentes quasi non sint, sic sunt coram eo,  et quasi nihilum et inane reputatæ sunt ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־הַגּוֹיִ֖ם כְּאַ֣יִן נֶגְדּ֑וֹ מֵאֶ֥פֶס וָתֹ֖הוּ נֶחְשְׁבוּ־לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: All nations are before him as if they had no being at all, and are counted to him as nothing, and vanity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki pakanat ovat niinkuin ei mitään hänen edessänsä, ja juuri tyhjän ja turhan edestä pidetään häneltä.
verses.king_james: All nations before him are as nothing; and they are counted to him less than nothing, and vanity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui ergo similem fecisti Deum ?  aut quam imaginem ponetis ei ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶל־מִ֖י תְּדַמְּי֣וּן אֵ֑ל וּמַה־דְּמ֖וּת תַּ֥עַרְכוּ לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: To whom then have you likened God? or what image will you make for him?
verses.finnish_biblia: Keneenkä te tahdotte verrata Jumalaa? eli mitä kuvaa te tahdotte hänelle tehdä?
verses.king_james: To whom then will ye liken God? or what likeness will ye compare unto him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid sculptile conflavit faber ?  aut aurifex auro figuravit illud,  et laminis argenteis argentarius ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַפֶּ֙סֶל֙ נָסַ֣ךְ חָרָ֔שׁ וְצֹרֵ֖ף בַּזָּהָ֣ב יְרַקְּעֶ֑נּוּ וּרְתֻק֥וֹת כֶּ֖סֶף צוֹרֵֽף׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hath the workman cast a graven statue? or hath the goldsmith formed it with gold, or the silversmith with plates of silver?
verses.finnish_biblia: Seppä valaa kuvan, ja hopiaseppä kultaa sen ja tekee siihen hopiakäädyt.
verses.king_james: The workman melteth a graven image, and the goldsmith spreadeth it over with gold, and casteth silver chains.

verses.latin_vulgate: Forte lignum et imputribile elegit ;  artifex sapiens quærit  quomodo statuat simulacrum, quod non moveatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַֽמְסֻכָּ֣ן תְּרוּמָ֔ה עֵ֥ץ לֹא־יִרְקַ֖ב יִבְחָ֑ר חָרָ֤שׁ חָכָם֙ יְבַקֶּשׁ־ל֔וֹ לְהָכִ֥ין פֶּ֖סֶל לֹ֥א יִמּֽוֹט׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: He hath chosen strong wood, and that will not rot: the skilful workman seeketh how he may set up an idol that may not be moved.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin myös jolla vähä vara on tehdä ylennysuhria, hän valitsee puun, joka ei mätäne, ja etsii siihen taitavan tekiän valmistamaan kuvaa, joka on kestäväinen.
verses.king_james: He that is so impoverished that he hath no oblation chooseth a tree that will not rot; he seeketh unto him a cunning workman to prepare a graven image, that shall not be moved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid non scitis ? numquid non audistis ?  numquid non annuntiatum est vobis ab initio ?  numquid non intellexistis fundamenta terræ ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲל֤וֹא תֵֽדְעוּ֙ הֲל֣וֹא תִשְׁמָ֔עוּ הֲל֛וֹא הֻגַּ֥ד מֵרֹ֖אשׁ לָכֶ֑ם הֲלוֹא֙ הֲבִ֣ינוֹתֶ֔ם מוֹסְד֖וֹת הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Do you not know? hath it not been heard? hath it not been told you from the beginning? have you not understood the foundations of the earth?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ettekö te tiedä? ettekö te kuule? eikö tämä ole ennen teille ilmoitettu? ettekö te ole sitä ymmärtäneet maan alusta?
verses.king_james: Have ye not known? have ye not heard? hath it not been told you from the beginning? have ye not understood from the foundations of the earth?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui sedet super gyrum terræ,  et habitatores ejus sunt quasi locustæ ;  qui extendit velut nihilum cælos,  et expandit eos sicut tabernaculum ad inhabitandum ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַיֹּשֵׁב֙ עַל־ח֣וּג הָאָ֔רֶץ וְיֹשְׁבֶ֖יהָ כַּחֲגָבִ֑ים הַנּוֹטֶ֤ה כַדֹּק֙ שָׁמַ֔יִם וַיִּמְתָּחֵ֥ם כָּאֹ֖הֶל לָשָֽׁבֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: It is he that sitteth upon the globe of the earth, and the inhabitants thereof are as locusts: he that stretcheth out the heavens as nothing, and spreadeth them out as a tent to dwell in.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän istuu maan piirin päällä, ja ne, jotka sen päällä asuvat, ovat niinkuin heinäsirkat; joka taivaan venyttää niinkuin ohukaisen nahan, ja levittää sen niinkuin teltan, asuttavaksi.
verses.king_james: It is he that sitteth upon the circle of the earth, and the inhabitants thereof are as grasshoppers; that stretcheth out the heavens as a curtain, and spreadeth them out as a tent to dwell in:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui dat secretorum scrutatores quasi non sint,  judices terræ velut inane fecit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַנּוֹתֵ֥ן רוֹזְנִ֖ים לְאָ֑יִן שֹׁ֥פְטֵי אֶ֖רֶץ כַּתֹּ֥הוּ עָשָֽׂה׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that bringeth the searchers of secrets to nothing, that hath made the judges of the earth as vanity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka pääruhtinaat tyhjäksi tekee, ja hävittää tuomarit maan päältä,
verses.king_james: That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quidem neque plantatus, neque satus,  neque radicatus in terra truncus eorum ;  repente flavit in eos, et aruerunt,  et turbo quasi stipulam auferet eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֣ף בַּל־נִטָּ֗עוּ אַ֚ף בַּל־זֹרָ֔עוּ אַ֛ף בַּל־שֹׁרֵ֥שׁ בָּאָ֖רֶץ גִּזְעָ֑ם וְגַם־נָשַׁ֤ף בָּהֶם֙ וַיִּבָ֔שׁוּ וּסְעָרָ֖ה כַּקַּ֥שׁ תִּשָּׂאֵֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And surely their stock was neither planted, nor sown, nor rooted in the earth: suddenly he hath blown upon them, and they are withered, and a whirlwind shall take them away as stubble.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin ei he olisi istutetut, eikä kylvetyt, eli heidän kantonsa juurtuneet maahan; niin että he kuivettuvat, kuin tuuli heidän päällensä puhaltaa, ja tuulispää vie heidät matkaansa niinkuin akanat.
verses.king_james: Yea, they shall not be planted; yea, they shall not be sown: yea, their stock shall not take root in the earth: and he shall also blow upon them, and they shall wither, and the whirlwind shall take them away as stubble.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cui assimilastis me, et adæquastis ?  dicit Sanctus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶל־מִ֥י תְדַמְּי֖וּנִי וְאֶשְׁוֶ֑ה יֹאמַ֖ר קָדֽוֹשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And to whom have ye likened me, or made me equal, saith the Holy One?
verses.finnish_biblia: Keneenkä te siis minun verrata tahdotte, jonka kaltainen minä olisin? sanoo Pyhä.
verses.king_james: To whom then will ye liken me, or shall I be equal? saith the Holy One.

verses.latin_vulgate: Levate in excelsum oculos vestros, et videte  quis creavit hæc :  qui educit in numero militiam eorum,  et omnes ex nomine vocat ;  præ multitudine fortitudinis et roboris, virtutisque ejus,  neque unum reliquum fuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׂאוּ־מָר֨וֹם עֵינֵיכֶ֤ם וּרְאוּ֙ מִֽי־בָרָ֣א אֵ֔לֶּה הַמּוֹצִ֥יא בְמִסְפָּ֖ר צְבָאָ֑ם לְכֻלָּם֙ בְּשֵׁ֣ם יִקְרָ֔א מֵרֹ֤ב אוֹנִים֙ וְאַמִּ֣יץ כֹּ֔חַ אִ֖ישׁ לֹ֥א נֶעְדָּֽר׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Lift up your eyes on high, and see who hath created these things: who bringeth out their host by number, and calleth them all by their names: by the greatness of his might, and strength, and power, not one of them was missing.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nostakaat silmänne korkeuteen ja katsokaat, kuka ne kappaleet luonut on, ja vie edes heidän joukkonsa luvulla? kutsuu ne kaikki nimeltänsä? Hänen varansa ja väkevä voimansa on niin suuri, ettei häneltä mitään puutu.
verses.king_james: Lift up your eyes on high, and behold who hath created these things, that bringeth out their host by number: he calleth them all by names by the greatness of his might, for that he is strong in power; not one faileth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare dicis, Jacob,  et loqueris, Israël :  Abscondita est via mea a Domino,  et a Deo meo judicium meum transivit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֤מָּה תֹאמַר֙ יַעֲקֹ֔ב וּתְדַבֵּ֖ר יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל נִסְתְּרָ֤ה דַרְכִּי֙ מֵיְהֹוָ֔ה וּמֵאֱלֹהַ֖י מִשְׁפָּטִ֥י יַעֲבֽוֹר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why sayest thou, O Jacob, and speakest, O Israel: My way is hid from the Lord, and my judgment is passed over from my God?
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksis siis sinä Jakob sanot, ja sinä Israel puhut: minun tieni on Herralta salattu, ja minun oikeuteni käy minun Jumalani ohitse?
verses.king_james: Why sayest thou, O Jacob, and speakest, O Israel, My way is hid from the Lord, and my judgment is passed over from my God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid nescis, aut non audisti ?  Deus sempiternus Dominus,  qui creavit terminos terræ :  non deficiet, neque laborabit,  nec est investigatio sapientiæ ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲל֨וֹא יָדַ֜עְתָּ אִם־לֹ֣א שָׁמַ֗עְתָּ אֱלֹהֵ֨י עוֹלָ֤ם ׀ יְהֹוָה֙ בּוֹרֵא֙ קְצ֣וֹת הָאָ֔רֶץ לֹ֥א יִיעַ֖ף וְלֹ֣א יִיגָ֑ע אֵ֥ין חֵ֖קֶר לִתְבוּנָתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Knowest thou not, or hast thou not heard? the Lord is the everlasting God, who hath created the ends of the earth: he shall not faint, nor labour, neither is there any searching out of his wisdom.
verses.finnish_biblia: Etkös tiedä? etkös ole kuullut? Herra ijankaikkinen Jumala, joka maan ääret on luonut, ei väsy eikä näänny; hänen ymmärryksensä on tutkimatoin.
verses.king_james: Hast thou not known? hast thou not heard, that the everlasting God, the Lord, the Creator of the ends of the earth, fainteth not, neither is weary? there is no searching of his understanding.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dat lasso virtutem,  et his qui non sunt, fortitudinem et robur multiplicat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֹתֵ֥ן לַיָּעֵ֖ף כֹּ֑חַ וּלְאֵ֥ין אוֹנִ֖ים עׇצְמָ֥ה יַרְבֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: It is he that giveth strength to the weary, and increaseth force and might to them that are not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän antaa väsyneille voiman, ja väettömille kyllä väkeä.
verses.king_james: He giveth power to the faint; and to them that have no might he increaseth strength.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deficient pueri, et laborabunt,  et juvenes in infirmitate cadent ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיִעֲפ֥וּ נְעָרִ֖ים וְיִגָ֑עוּ וּבַחוּרִ֖ים כָּשׁ֥וֹל יִכָּשֵֽׁלוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: You shall faint, and labour, and young men shall fall by infirmity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nuorukaiset väsyvät ja nääntyvät, ja nuoret miehet peräti lankeevat;
verses.king_james: Even the youths shall faint and be weary, and the young men shall utterly fall:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui autem sperant in Domino mutabunt fortitudinem,  assument pennas sicut aquilæ :  current et non laborabunt,  ambulabunt et non deficient.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְקוֹיֵ֤ יְהֹוָה֙ יַחֲלִ֣יפוּ כֹ֔חַ יַעֲל֥וּ אֵ֖בֶר כַּנְּשָׁרִ֑ים יָר֙וּצוּ֙ וְלֹ֣א יִיגָ֔עוּ יֵלְכ֖וּ וְלֹ֥א יִיעָֽפוּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: But they that hope in the Lord shall renew their strength, they shall take wings as eagles, they shall run and not be weary, they shall walk and not faint.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta jotka Herraa odottavat, ne saavat uuden voiman, niin että he menevät siivillä ylös kuin kotkat; että he juoksevat ja ei näänny, he vaeltavat ja ei väsy.
verses.king_james: But they that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not faint.
